sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, data VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP(6));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at) VALUES (1, 'Sample text', timestamp(6) '2023-01-01 12:00:00.000000');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at) VALUES (2, '', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at) VALUES (3, 'Edge case entry', timestamp(6) '2023-01-01 00:00:00.000000');
SELECT substr(data, 1) AS first_char, substr(data, 2) AS second_char FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, substr(data, 3) AS third_char FROM test_table WHERE created_at = timestamp(6) '2023-01-01 12:00:00.000000';
SELECT t1.id, t1.data, substr(t2.data, 1) AS first_char FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id + 1;
SELECT id, data, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY substr(data, 1) ) AS count_per_char FROM test_table;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM test_table GROUP BY substr(data, 1) HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, substr(data, 1) FROM (SELECT id, data FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL) AS subquery WHERE substr(data, 1) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT CASE WHEN data IS NULL THEN 'NULL' ELSE substr(data, 1) END AS test FROM test_table;
SELECT id, substr(data, 2) FROM test_table WHERE created_at = timestamp(6) '2023-01-01 00:00:00.000000' OR data = '' ORDER BY substr(data, 2);
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, string_data VARCHAR(255), created_date TIMESTAMP(6));
INSERT INTO another_table (id, string_data, created_date) VALUES (10, 'Another string', timestamp(6) '2023-01-02 14:00:00.123456');
SELECT a.id, substr(a.string_data, 1) AS first_char FROM another_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.id = b.id WHERE b.data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT a.id, COUNT(*) FROM another_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.id = b.id GROUP BY substr(b.data, 1) HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
